21.correct: According to tradition, the great Sanskrit poet Kalidasa flourished in the court of Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya).

wrong.A: Skandagupta was a later Gupta ruler.

21.fact: Kalidasa is famed for works like 'Abhijnanasakuntalam' and 'Meghaduta'.

22.correct: As per the 2011 Census, Mumbai (Greater Mumbai) was the largest metropolitan city by population.

wrong.A: Kolkata is large but smaller than Mumbai.
wrong.B: Delhi is large but the 2011 figure for Greater Mumbai was higher.

22.fact: The Census 2011 recorded Greater Mumbai's population at over 1.2 crore.

23.correct: The Government of India Act, 1858 marked the official end of East India Company rule, transferring power to the British Crown.

wrong.A: The Regulating Act, 1773 began parliamentary control but did not end Company rule.
wrong.C: The 1861 Act dealt with legislative councils.
wrong.D: The 1833 Charter Act centralised the Company's administration.

23.fact: After 1858 India was governed by the Crown through a Secretary of State for India.

24.correct: The PM Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (approved July 2025) has an annual allocation of about Rs 24,000 crore.
24.fact: The scheme targets 100 low-performing agricultural districts over six years.

25.correct: Mazagon Dock was taken over by the Government of India in 1960 (it became a public-sector shipbuilder).
25.fact: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders builds warships and submarines for the Indian Navy.

26.correct: The pressure belt between about 30 and 35 degrees latitude, also called the 'horse latitudes', is the Subtropical High Pressure Belt.

wrong.A: The Equatorial Low lies near the equator.
wrong.C: The Subpolar Low lies around 60 degrees latitude.
wrong.D: The Polar High lies near the poles.

26.fact: The subtropical highs are zones of descending air, associated with the world's great deserts.

27.correct: Under PM-DAKSH, OBC and EWS candidates with an annual family income below Rs 3 lakh are eligible for free training.

wrong.B: 5 lakh is not the income ceiling for these categories under this provision.

27.fact: PM-DAKSH provides free skill training to marginalised groups.

28.correct: The traditional dance form 'Gaudiya Nritya' is practised in West Bengal.

wrong.B: It is not from Jammu and Kashmir.
wrong.D: It is not from Andhra Pradesh.

28.fact: Gaudiya Nritya is a classical dance tradition revived from ancient Bengal (Gauda).

29.correct: The famous 'Panna Diamond Mines' are located in Madhya Pradesh.
29.fact: Panna is India's only significant diamond-producing area.

30.correct: In West Bengal, the festival of Holi is known as 'Dol Purnima' (or Dol Jatra).

wrong.B: Bhai Phonta is the Bengali version of Bhai Dooj.
wrong.C: Jagaddhatri Puja is a separate festival.
wrong.D: Kalpataru Utsav is a Ramakrishna Mission observance.

30.fact: On Dol Purnima, idols of Radha and Krishna are placed on a decorated swing (dola).

31.correct: The change of state from liquid to gas at atmospheric pressure is called vaporisation (evaporation/boiling).
31.fact: Vaporisation occurs either as slow evaporation or rapid boiling at the boiling point.

32.correct: Gender budgeting was introduced in India in 2005-2006.

wrong.A: 1991-92 was the year of economic reforms, not gender budgeting.

32.fact: Gender budgeting analyses the budget for its impact on women and allocates funds accordingly.

33.correct: India won its first men's Cricket World Cup in 1983, under captain Kapil Dev.

wrong.B: 1975 was the first-ever World Cup (won by West Indies).

33.fact: India beat the West Indies in the 1983 World Cup final at Lord's.

34.correct: Both statements are correct - critical minerals are the building blocks of the modern economy (I) and are increasingly called 'the oil of the 21st century' (II) - so 'Both I and II'.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are correct.

34.fact: Critical minerals like lithium and cobalt are vital for batteries and clean-energy technology.

35.correct: The 20th Asian Games are to be held in 2026 at Aichi-Nagoya, Japan.
35.fact: The Asian Games are held every four years under the Olympic Council of Asia.

36.correct: The Secretary-General of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) is appointed for a term of 3 years.

wrong.C: 5 years is the UN Secretary-General's term, not the SCO's.

36.fact: The SCO is a Eurasian political, economic and security organisation.

37.correct: Both mass transport (I) and logistics infrastructure (II) are driving sectors of the PM Gati Shakti initiative, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are covered.

37.fact: PM Gati Shakti is a national master plan for multi-modal, integrated infrastructure connectivity.

38.correct: Both the breaststroke (I) and the backstroke (II) are recognised swimming strokes, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are swimming strokes.

38.fact: The four competitive swimming strokes are freestyle, backstroke, breaststroke and butterfly.

39.correct: The Environmental Performance Index (EPI) 2024 uses 58 performance indicators.
39.fact: The EPI is produced by Yale and Columbia universities, ranking countries on environmental health and ecosystem vitality.

40.correct: In India, the Comprehensive Environmental Pollution Index (CEPI) is published by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB).

wrong.B: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) is a judicial body, not the publisher.
wrong.C: The Central Ground Water Board deals with groundwater.
wrong.D: NITI Aayog does not publish the CEPI.

40.fact: The CEPI is used to identify and monitor critically polluted industrial areas.
